ThinkPHP from Scratch Day 14 - Model Basics, Part 2: Inserting Data
Last time we got most of the Model boilerplate ready. Now it's time to actually start writing some basic data operations.
Last post we already built a
controller method, but right now all it does is pull the form data the frontend sends to the backend. Now we actually want to save that data into the
table we created. First, we need to add a line up top telling the Controller which Model we want to use:

I got stuck in a weird spot while writing this, to the point where I had no choice but to reinstall the database — burned almost half an hour on it. Next post, we'll get into querying data~

Last time we got all the Model boilerplate ready. Now it's time to actually write some basic data operations. In this post we wire up a Model inside the controller, instantiate it, and insert a real row into the think_user table — then check whether the save actually worked using the boolean that save() returns.
